methods.description: Process for isolating and preparing Moyeria specimens from rock samples for microscopic analysis.

steps.description: Specimens of Moyeria were isolated from fresh rock collected in the field by CHW and PKS.
steps.searchable_summary: collect rock samples containing Moyeria specimens

steps.description: Rock samples were cleaned and crushed and 40 g was demineralised using standard palynological hydrochloric acid (HCl)-hydrofluoric acid (HF)-HCl acid maceration techniques.
steps.searchable_summary: demineralize rock samples using acid maceration

steps.description: The residue was sieved through a 20-mm mesh.
A heavy liquid (zinc bromide) separation was then undertaken to remove any remaining undissolved minerals, followed by further sieving using a 20-lm mesh.
steps.searchable_summary: perform heavy liquid separation and sieve through 20-lm mesh

steps.description: Some of the organic residue was mounted on glass slides using epoxy resin for LM analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: mount organic residue on glass slides with epoxy resin

steps.description: The remaining residue was examined under a dissecting microscope and individual specimens picked for TEM analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: examine residue under microscope and pick specimens for TEM

steps.description: Picked specimens were embedded in a small agar block and then embedded in epoxy resin using standard preparatory techniques.
steps.searchable_summary: embed specimens in agar and epoxy resin

steps.description: Sections were cut using an ultramicrotome and imaged using a JOEL 2010 transmitting electron microscope operating at 80 kV.
steps.searchable_summary: cut sections with ultramicrotome and image with TEM
